    About Me

    On this page

    • What I specialize in
    • Current focus
    • Selected strengths
    Content workflow architecture · metadata · validation · developer enablement

    Hi, I’m Anusha Jeyaseelan

    I’m a senior content workflow architect and staff-level content strategist with 10+ years of experience building scalable content systems for cloud, security, networking, APIs, infrastructure, and AI-enabled platforms.

    My work goes beyond traditional technical writing. I design the operating model behind technical content and reusable developer assets: how they are requested, structured, reviewed, validated, published, discovered, maintained, and reused.

    I specialize in creating contributor-ready systems that combine metadata models, taxonomy, readiness criteria, publishing standards, lifecycle governance, and practical guidance for technical users.

    Contributor workflows Metadata models Validation readiness Publishing systems Developer enablement

    What I specialize in

    Workflow architecture

    Designing repeatable systems for intake, authoring, review, validation, publishing, lifecycle updates, and contributor enablement.

    Metadata & discovery

    Creating taxonomy, structured content profiles, metadata standards, search readiness patterns, and reusable content models.

    Developer platforms

    Building API references, SDK examples, integration guides, executable samples, internal knowledge bases, and self-service documentation.

    Publishing systems

    Improving Git-based authoring, docs-as-code workflows, CI/CD publishing, automated reviews, and release-aligned content operations.

    Current focus

    I’m expanding my OpenUSD and Omniverse knowledge with a focus on how reusable digital twin assets are structured, packaged, validated, published, discovered, and maintained. This connects closely with my background in metadata, validation, contributor enablement, publishing workflows, and reusable developer content systems.

    OpenUSD learning Digital twin asset workflows Validation readiness Reusable technical assets

    Selected strengths

    • Turning complex technical workflows into repeatable contributor-ready systems
    • Defining standards that improve consistency, discoverability, reuse, and lifecycle quality
    • Partnering with engineering, product, program, support, and content teams
    • Creating workflow guidance, templates, examples, and enablement material for technical users
